data.title: Prolocor to Present New Clinical Evidence at ACC 25 Supporting Use of PLATELET FCɣRIIA (PFCG) As Key Tool In Individualizing Dual Antiplatelet Therapy (DAPT)
data.description:
Date: March 31, 2025, 10:36 A.M.

Location: Moderated Poster Theater 7

PROGNOSTIC IMPLICATIONS OF PLATELET FCɣRIIA DURING THE FIRST YEAR AFTER MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION

PHILADELPHIA, PA / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Prolocor, Inc., a healthcare startup developing the innovative diagnostic Prolocor pFCG™ test that identifies patients at higher and lower risk of thrombotic events (heart attack, stroke, and death), announced today the acceptance of its abstract entitled PROGNOSTIC IMPLICATIONS OF PLATELET FCɣRIIA DURING THE FIRST YEAR AFTER MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION as a moderated poster presentation at The American College of Cardiology's 74th Annual Scientific Session & Expo in Chicago on March 31, 2025.

Prolocor Logo

Prolocor Logo

David J. Schneider, MD, FACC FAHA, Co-Founder and Chief Science Officer of Prolocor, stated, "I am eager to present these compelling results at ACC 25 to colleagues from around the world. I also extend my gratitude to those participating in our ongoing clinical program."

"We are privileged to participate in ACC 25 and contribute to discussions on the latest advancements in science and innovation in this field. The importance of individualized antiplatelet therapy in patient care is increasing, and we are proud to be at the forefront of this development," added Peter M. DiBattiste, MD, FACC, FAHA, Co-Founder and CEO of Prolocor.

Prolocor is also pleased to announce that it was accepted to display in the American College of Cardiology's Future Hub. The goal of the Future Hub is to inform, educate, inspire and delight attendees of the Chicago Session & Expo by exposing them to the latest innovations in digital health, medical devices and big data. Prolocor looks forward to participating in this important event. Please stop by booth FH21.

ABOUT PROLOCOR, INC.

Founded by a team that deeply understands thrombosis and cardiovascular disease, Prolocor is building its strategy around platelet FcγRIIa and embarking on a journey to commercialize an innovative precision diagnostic test that quantifies FcγRIIa on the surface of platelets. For more information on Prolocor, please visit the company's website at www.prolocor.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This press release contains forward-looking statements as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, as amended. Forward-looking statements are statements that are not historical facts. These statements include projections and estimates and their underlying assumptions, statements regarding plans, objectives, intentions and expectations with respect to future financial results, events, operations, services, product development and potential, and statements regarding future performance. Forward-looking statements are generally identified by the words "expects," "anticipates," "believes," "intends," "estimates," "plans" and similar expressions. Although Prolocor's management believes that the expectations reflected in such forward-looking statements are reasonable, forward-looking information and statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties, many of which are difficult to predict and generally beyond the control of Prolocor, that could cause actual results and developments to differ materially from those expressed in, or implied or projected by, the forward-looking information and statements. These risks and uncertainties include among other things, Prolocor's planned level of revenues and capital expenditures, Prolocor's available cash and its ability to obtain additional funding, the uncertainties inherent in research and development, future clinical data and analysis, including post marketing, decisions by regulatory authorities regarding whether and when to approve any device or application that may be filed for any such product candidates and other matters that could affect the availability or commercial potential of such product candidates, the fact that product candidates if approved may not be commercially successful, the future approval and commercial success of therapeutic alternatives, Prolocor's ability to benefit from external growth opportunities, to complete related transactions and/or obtain regulatory clearances, and risks associated with intellectual property. Other than as required by applicable law, Prolocor does not undertake any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking information or statements.

Contact Information

Ray Russo

Prolocor Inc

ray.russo@prolocor.com

data.title: TDOC INVESTIGATION: Teladoc Health, Inc. Investigated For Securities Fraud; Block & Leviton Encourages Investors Who Have Lost Money to Contact the Firm
data.description:
BOSTON, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Block & Leviton is investigating Teladoc Health, Inc. for potential securities law violations. Investors who have lost money in their Teladoc Health, Inc. investment should contact the firm to learn more about how they might recover those losses. For more details, visit https://blockleviton.com/cases/tdoc.

What is this all about?

Shares of Teladoc Health fell over 12% on February 20 following the publication of a Blue Orca Capital report alleging the company has misled investors about its BetterHelp platform and financial reporting. The report claims that BetterHelp has used AI-generated responses in place of licensed therapists without proper disclosures to investors and patients and, among other allegations, the report accuses Teladoc of inflating profitability by shifting R&D expenses. Block & Leviton is investigating.

Who is eligible?

Anyone who purchased Teladoc Health, Inc. common stock and has seen their shares fall may be eligible, whether or not they have sold their investment. Investors should contact Block & Leviton to learn more.

What is Block & Leviton doing?

Block & Leviton is investigating whether the Company committed securities law violations and may file an action to attempt to recover losses on behalf of investors who have lost money.

What should you do next?

If you've lost money on your investment, you should contact Block & Leviton to learn more via our case website, by email at shareholders@blockleviton.com, or by phone at (888) 256-2510.

Whistleblower?

If you have non-public information about Teladoc Health, Inc., you should consider assisting in our investigation or working with our attorneys to file a report with the Securities Exchange Commission under their whistleblower program. Whistleblowers who provide original information to the SEC may receive rewards of up to 30% of any successful recovery. For more information, contact Block & Leviton at shareholders@blockleviton.com or by phone at (888) 256-2510.

Why should you contact Block & Leviton?

Block & Leviton is widely regarded as one of the leading securities class action firms in the country. Our attorneys have recovered billions of dollars for defrauded investors and are dedicated to obtaining significant recoveries on behalf of our clients through active litigation in the federal courts across the country. Many of the nation's top institutional investors hire us to represent their interests. You can learn more about us at our website www.blockleviton.com, call (888) 256-2510 or email shareholders@blockleviton.com with any questions.

This notice may constitute attorney advertising.

CONTACT:

BLOCK & LEVITON LLP

260 Franklin St., Suite 1860

Boston, MA 02110

Phone: (888) 256-2510

Email: shareholders@blockleviton.com

data.title: Sylvamo Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Sylvamo Corporation, a leading global producer of uncoated freesheet papers and market pulp, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. Sylvamo operates across three main geographical segments: Europe, Latin America, and North America, and continues to focus on enhancing its production capacity and market position.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $3,773 million, reflecting a slight increase from $3,721 million in 2023, driven by higher volumes despite lower sales price and mix.
  • Income From Continuing Operations Before Income Taxes: $405 million, up from $369 million in 2023, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $302 million, compared to $253 million in 2023, showcasing strong financial performance and effective cost management.
  • Earnings Per Share from Continuing Operations - Diluted: $7.18, an increase from $5.93 in 2023, highlighting enhanced shareholder value.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in three main geographical segments: Europe, Latin America, and North America. In 2024, Europe generated $799 million in sales, Latin America $945 million, and North America $2,029 million.
  • Geographical Performance - Europe: The Europe segment saw a decrease in sales by $20 million in 2024 compared to 2023, primarily due to lower sales prices and mix, despite higher volumes. The operating profit improved by $35 million due to lower maintenance outages and input costs.
  • Geographical Performance - Latin America: Sales in Latin America decreased by $32 million in 2024, driven by lower sales prices and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts. Operating profit decreased by $47 million due to higher operating costs and maintenance outages.
  • Geographical Performance - North America: North America experienced a sales increase of $78 million in 2024, driven by higher volumes. Operating profit increased by $24 million due to lower unabsorbed costs and input costs, despite lower sales prices and higher operating costs.
  • New Production Launches: On January 2, 2023, the company completed the acquisition of Stora Enso’s uncoated freesheet paper mill in Nymölla, Sweden, enhancing its production capacity in Europe.
  • Future Outlook: The company remains committed to generating strong adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow in 2025, with plans to continue returning cash to shareholders and investing in high-return capital projects.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Acquisitions and Divestitures: Sylvamo completed the acquisition of Stora Enso’s uncoated freesheet paper mill in Nymölla, Sweden, to enhance its production capacity and strengthen its market position in Europe. The company also divested its Russian operations to streamline its business and focus on core markets.
  • Capital Management: Sylvamo repaid $154 million in debt and returned $130 million in cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. The company issued new debt, including Term Loan F-2, and utilized its Revolving Credit Facility and AR Securitization to manage liquidity. Capital expenditures totaled $221 million, with a focus on maintenance, regulatory compliance, and high-return projects.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead to 2025, Sylvamo aims to continue generating strong adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow while returning cash to shareholders. The company plans to invest approximately $50 to $70 million in high-return projects and maintain annual maintenance, regulatory, and reforestation capital expenditures in the range of $175 to $190 million.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic and Political Conditions: Sylvamo operates in Europe, Latin America, and North America, and is significantly affected by global and regional economic and political conditions. Adverse conditions, such as recessions, inflation, and military conflicts, could reduce demand for products, increase costs, and disrupt operations.
  • Climate Change and Environmental Risks: The company is exposed to physical, financial, and reputational risks associated with climate change. Severe weather events, regulatory changes, and increased focus on sustainability could disrupt operations and increase costs.
  • Public Health Crises: A public health crisis could reduce demand for products, disrupt operations, and increase costs due to labor shortages and supply chain disruptions.
  • Industry Cyclicality: The paper and pulp industry is cyclical, with demand and prices fluctuating based on economic conditions and industry capacity. Declines in demand or increases in costs could adversely affect profitability.
  • Raw Material and Energy Costs: Changes in the cost or availability of raw materials and energy could increase production costs and impact operations. The company experienced inflation in wood fiber costs in 2024.
  • Transportation Disruptions: Reduced availability of transportation could lead to higher costs and affect the timely delivery of products, impacting sales and reputation.
  • Decline in Paper Demand: The industry-wide decline in demand for paper products due to digital alternatives could materially affect revenue and growth opportunities.
  • Competition and Industry Consolidation: Increased competition and consolidation within the paper industry could impact Sylvamo's competitive position and financial performance.
  • Operational Disruptions: Material disruptions at manufacturing facilities due to natural disasters, equipment failures, or other operational issues could significantly impact operations.
  • Cybersecurity and Technology Risks: The company faces risks related to cybersecurity breaches and the increasing use of artificial intelligence, which could lead to data breaches and operational disruptions.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Compliance with extensive environmental and other regulations could result in significant costs. Changes in laws and regulations could further impact operations and financial performance.
  • Customer Concentration: Reliance on a small number of significant customers exposes Sylvamo to risks related to customer financial viability and purchasing habits.
  • Capital Requirements: The company's operations require substantial capital investments, which could affect cash flows and financial flexibility.
  • Labor Challenges: Attracting and retaining skilled employees is critical, and labor shortages or disputes could disrupt operations.
  • Goodwill Impairment: A significant write-down of goodwill or other intangible assets could adversely affect financial results.
  • Pension Plan Risks: Changes in investment returns, interest rates, or plan demographics could impact pension plan funding and financial condition.
  • Indebtedness: The company's debt levels could limit financial flexibility and increase vulnerability to economic conditions.

SEC Filing: Sylvamo Corp [ SLVM ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: INNOVATIVE DESIGNS INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Innovative Designs Inc., a company specializing in house wrap for the building construction industry and cold weather clothing, has released its annual 10-K report. The report highlights significant financial improvements, operational achievements, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks faced by the company over the past year.

Financial Highlights

Revenues, net: $1.38 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year's $0.35 million, driven primarily by the House Wrap product line.

Cost of sales: $0.75 million, increased due to higher sales volume.

Income (loss) from operations: $0.13 million, compared to a loss of $0.28 million in the previous year, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Net income (loss): $0.10 million, a substantial improvement from the previous year's net loss of $0.30 million.

Net income (loss) per common share - undiluted: $0.002, compared to a loss of $0.010 per share in the previous year, reflecting the company's return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates in two main segments: house wrap for the building construction industry and cold weather clothing. The house wrap segment, which includes products with R-3 and R-6 value insulation, accounted for a significant portion of the revenue, with major customers like A-Team Building Supplies, LLC and Built Link Solutions contributing 10.3% and 77% of sales, respectively.

Product Lines: The company offers several products containing Insultex, including House Wrap, Arctic Armor Line, and Insultex Material. The Arctic Armor Line, which includes jackets, bibs, and gloves, is designed for extreme cold weather and provides buoyancy and thermal resistance.

Sales Channels: Products are sold through the company's website, retailers, distributors, and independent sales agents across the United States and Canada. The primary market area is the outdoor industry, which includes activities such as hunting, ice fishing, and snowmobiling.

Geographical Performance: The company's products are distributed in all states and Canada, with domestic distributors covering regions such as Texas, Oklahoma, and New York, among others.

Operational Challenges: The company faced a temporary suspension of advertising for its House Wrap product line due to litigation with the FTC, which was resolved in favor of the company, allowing them to resume advertising in March 2021.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue funding operations through revenues, private borrowings, and the sale of common stock until it can produce sales sufficient to cover its cost structure or secure commercial lending arrangements. There is also an intention to put the Insultex production equipment into operation once sufficient funding is available.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Innovative Designs, Inc. is focused on expanding its production capabilities by planning to put into production equipment capable of producing its own Insultex material, once sufficient funding is secured. The company is also working on obtaining ICC-ES certification for its House Wrap product to secure larger orders, which is a strategic move to enhance market opportunities.

Capital Management: The company funded its operations through revenues, private sales of common stock, and stock issuance for services, raising a total of $115,920 from stock sales. It also managed its debt obligations by making payments on loans and notes payable. The company has a significant reliance on shareholder loans and private borrowings due to limited access to commercial lending facilities.

Future Outlook: Innovative Designs, Inc. plans to continue funding operations through revenues, private borrowings, and potential sales of securities. The company aims to achieve sales sufficient to cover its cost structure and secure commercial lending arrangements. Additionally, the company is focused on rebuilding its House Wrap product line brand and exploring opportunities to enhance its financial position through strategic partnerships and certifications.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: The company operates in two segments: house wrap for the building construction industry and cold weather clothing, both utilizing Insultex material. A significant challenge is the dependency on a single supplier for Insultex, which could disrupt production if supply issues arise. Additionally, the company is financially constrained, impacting its ability to bring Insultex production equipment into operation, which is crucial for reducing reliance on external suppliers.

Risk Factors:

  • Lack of Sufficient Operating Funds: The company is concerned about cash flow issues due to unfavorable payment terms with manufacturers, which could necessitate purchase order financing or loans.
  • Competition: The markets are highly competitive, with larger competitors like Dupont and Kimberly Clark having more resources, potentially leading to loss of market share.
  • Cyclicality: Sales of Arctic Armor apparel are dependent on cold weather, making revenues seasonal and unpredictable.
  • Material Acquisition: Dependency on a single supplier for Insultex and a single manufacturer in Indonesia poses a risk of supply chain disruptions.
  • Geographic Concentration: Sales are concentrated in colder climates, limiting market reach.
  • Penny Stock Considerations: The company's stock is classified as a penny stock, which may limit trading activity and liquidity due to regulatory requirements.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: The company has seen a significant increase in revenue, primarily from the House Wrap product line. However, the cost of goods sold has also increased substantially, impacting profit margins. Management is focused on rebuilding the House Wrap brand and securing ICC-ES certification to enhance marketability. The company is funding operations through revenues, private stock sales, and legal settlements, but lacks access to commercial lending, which could affect its ability to fulfill purchase orders.

SEC Filing: INNOVATIVE DESIGNS INC [ IVDN ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: Hims & Hers Stock Jumps 17.5% After Acquiring At-Home Lab Testing Facility
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health Inc. (HIMS, Financials) surged 17.5% to $68.74 on Wednesday following the announcement of its acquisition of Sigmund NJ LLC, marketed as Trybe Labs, to introduce at-home lab testing. Pre-market activity added another 1.31% to the stock, which as of early Thursday came to $69.64.

As part of its larger customized healthcare strategy, the purchase allows Hims & Hers to provide more testing servicesincluding at-home blood draws. Using a blood lancet, the new testing capabilities will let consumers evaluate hormone levels, heart risk, stress indicators, cholesterol, liver function, thyroid function, and prostate health, therefore removing the need for conventional needles or dried blood spot cards. Using this information, healthcare practitioners on the site will lead individualized treatment regimens.

According to Hims & Hers, the purchase fits their approach of entering high-impact therapeutic areas such as low testosterone, perimenopause, and menopause support. The firm wants to include lab testing into its current line of customized treatments, vitamins, and drugs.

De-identified lab data will also help Hims & Hersan artificial intelligence-driven platform for tailored healthcare recommendationsdevelop MedMatch. Within the following year, the business hopes to provide at-home testing services to consumers.

Hims & Hers paid for the purchase using available cash on hand. Now in the ownership of the corporation, the New Jersey facility

data.title: Rice Futures at Over 1-Week Low
data.description:
Rice futures traded below $14 per hundredweight, their lowest in over a week, due to ample supplies from major producers in Asia while demand remains sluggish.

Rice stocks in top producer India reached a fresh record level of 67.6 million tonnes at the start of February, compared to the government's target of 7.6 million tons, data compiled by the Food Corporation of India (FCI) showed.

Concurrently, Vietnam plans to export over 7.5 million tons of rice in 2025, mostly from the Mekong Delta region, which is expected to produce around 24 million tons of rice this year, according to the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Department of Agriculture's February WASDE report revised U.S. rice production for 2024/25 upwards to 222.1 million cwt, an increase of 2.3 million from the prior month, with significant gains in Texas and Missouri.

data.title: Stocks of U.S. Ethanol Grow
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

Inventories of ethanol in the U.S. rose through the week ended Feb. 14, said the Energy Information Administration in its latest report.

In Thursday's report, the EIA said ethanol inventories totaled 26.22 million barrels for the week, up nearly 530,000 barrels from the previous week. It's also above the estimates of analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal this week, with those analysts projecting inventories anywhere from 25.39 million barrels to 26 million barrels.

Weekly average production, meanwhile, inched up from the previous week--landing at 1.084 million barrels a day, up 2,000 barrels from last week's report.

Most-active corn futures trading on the CBOT are up 0.3% in afternoon trading, bringing the price back up to $5 a bushel.

To see related data, search "U.S. DOE Oil Data: PADD Breakdown" in Dow Jones NewsPlus.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

STOREBRAND ASA - Change in Board of Directors
data.description:
Storebrand ASA

With great sorrow, the Board of Directors and management of Storebrand received the news of Marianne Bergmann Røren's passing on February 18th, after a courageous battle with cancer. Marianne was a skilled and dedicated business leader with deep insight into both our industry and the business world in general. Since 2020, she has been a valued member of the Board of Directors at Storebrand ASA. During these years, she successfully built a strong bridge between strategic, commercial, and regulatory work.

Marianne had an impressive career, most recently as the CEO of Mesta AS. We will remember Marianne for her strong commitment, warm personality, and unique ability to inspire and lead. Her contributions to Storebrand and the business community will be deeply missed. Our thoughts are with her family and loved ones during this difficult time.

Until the upcoming ordinary annual general meeting is held, Storebrand will continue with the remaining board which is in accordance with the company's article of association.

Lysaker, 20 February 2025

For further inquiries, please contact:

Johannes Narum, Head of Investor Relations:

johannes.narum@storebrand.no or (+47) 993 33 569

https://news.cision.com/storebrand-asa/r/storebrand-asa---change-in-board-of-directors%2Cc4109164

data.title: Cricket-India captain Rohit plans dinner atonement for ruining Axar's hat-trick
data.description:
India captain Rohit Sharma plans to take teammate Axar Patel to dinner to make amends after denying the spinner a hat-trick in Thursday's Champions Trophy match against Bangladesh.

Left-arm spinner Axar struck in his first over dismissing Tanzid Hasan and Mushfiqur Rahim with successive deliveries with wicketkeeper KL Rahul taking both the catches.

The crowd at the Dubai International Stadium was abuzz with the prospect of a hat-trick and Axar duly induced an edge from Jaker Ali, which flew to slip towards Rohit.

The India captain, however, floored the simple catch and was seen slapping the ground in frustration before getting up and apologising to Axar with folded hands.

"(I) may take him for a dinner tomorrow," a smiling Rohit said after India kicked off their campaign with a six-wicket victory.

"That was an easy catch, I should have taken that catch.

"You know the standard that I have set for myself standing in the slips, that was a little disappointing. But again these things happen, I do understand that."

Bangladesh were reeling at 35-5 but India dropped two catches and a stumping opportunity to allow Jaker (68) combine with Towhid Hridoy (100) in a 154-run stand.

"Credit to Hridoy and Jaker Ali. They played brilliantly to stitch that big partnership," Rohit said.

A cramped-up Hridoy batted practically on one leg towards the end of his gallant knock.

"The way Hridoy and Jaker batted, I think it was impressive batting, especially on this kind of wicket," Bangladesh captain Najmul Hossain Shanto said.

"The ball was spinning, but the way they handled the condition was brilliant to see.

"I hope they will continue (to bat like this) in the next matches."

India next face Pakistan on Sunday, while Bangladesh will meet Group A leader New Zealand in Rawalpindi on Monday.

data.title: Vedanta's Shareholders and Creditors Strongly Endorse its Demerger Proposal with Over 99% Favorable Votes
data.description:
Vedanta's Shareholders and Creditors Strongly Endorse its Demerger Proposal with Over 99% Favorable Votes

MUMBAI, India--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 20, 2025--

Vedanta Limited ("Vedanta") announced in an exchange filing that the resolutions listed in its Notice of Equity Shareholders, Secured Creditors, and Unsecured Creditors dated January 17, 2025 have been passed with the requisite majority at their respective meetings held on February 18, 2025.

The resolutions sought the approval of the shareholders, secured, and unsecured creditors on Vedanta's proposed demerger scheme.

  • Resolution seeking approval of shareholders: approved with 99.9987% of votes in favour on votes cast.
  • Resolution seeking approval of secured creditors: approved with 99.5900 % of the valid votes cast.
  • Resolution seeking approval of unsecured creditors: approved by 99.9588% of the of the valid votes cast.

The demerger will result in the creation of five independent companies:

  • Vedanta Aluminium
  • Vedanta Oil & Gas
  • Vedanta Power
  • Vedanta Iron and Steel
  • Vedanta Limited

The proposed demerger scheme will remain subject to receipt of other applicable statutory, government and regulatory approvals including inter alia from India's National Company Law Tribunal.

Vedanta is one of the world's leading critical and transition minerals, energy and technology companies spanning India, South Africa, Namibia, Liberia, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Korea, Taiwan and Japan with significant operations in Oil & Gas, Zinc, Lead, Silver, Copper, Iron Ore, Steel, Nickel, Aluminium, Power, Glass Substrate and display glass. The Group is also foraying into electronics manufacturing. Vedanta has governance and sustainable development at the core of its strategy.

Vedanta is ranked among the top 5 in the S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment 2024 and has been listed in the Dow Jones Sustainability World Index. The company has also been certified as a Great Place to Work and Kincentric Best Employer 2023. Vedanta Limited is listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) of India.

More information on Vedanta's demerger scheme can be found here: Investor Relations page.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250220050607/en/

data.title: The Gardner School of Chicago-Ravenswood Earns National Accreditation for Excellence in Early Childhood Education
data.description:
Chicago, IL, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The Gardner School of Ravenswood, which serves children ages six weeks to 5 years, has earned accreditation from the National Association for the Education of Young Children (NAEYC) – the world’s largest organization working on behalf of young children.

NAEYC accreditation is a rigorous quality-improvement system that uses a set of 10 research-based standards to recognize high-quality early education programs. In the 30 years since NAEYC accreditation has been established, less than 10% of all childcare centers, preschools and kindergartens nationally have achieved this recognition. 

“This prestigious accreditation reflects The Gardner School of Chicago-Ravenswood’s commitment to academic excellence, safety and exceptional early childhood education,” said Emily Davidson, School Director of Ravenswood. “Every day, we strive to create an enriching learning environment that nurtures curiosity, confidence, and a lifelong love of learning. Earning this recognition from NAEYC reinforces our commitment to providing exceptional educational experiences for young children and their families.”

To achieve NAEYC accreditation, The Gardner School of Chicago-Ravenswood completed a comprehensive evaluation process, which included rigorous assessments and unannounced on-site visits by NAEYC assessors. These evaluations ensure the school consistently upholds high standards in curriculum, staff qualifications, and parent-teacher collaboration, meeting or exceeding the highest professional benchmarks for early childhood education.

Ravenswood is The Gardner School’s 11th campus in the Chicago area to earn NAEYC accreditation following its preschools in the communities of Bucktown, Lincoln Park, West Loop, Northbrook, Oak Brook, Lombard, Schaumburg, Lincolnshire, Naperville and River North.

Headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee, The Gardner School operates 39 locations across eight states and the District of Columbia. Led by highly qualified educators, each location incorporates research-based curriculum and child-directed play to support the students’ academic, social, and emotional growth. 

The Gardner School of Ravenswood offers state-of-the-art facilities where students benefit from on-site academically focused learning with supplemental enrichment classes like foreign languages, karate, music and arts and outdoor playscape.

“At The Gardner School, we know future academic success begins in early childhood with a strong foundation built through high-quality curriculum and personalized learning experiences,” said Regional Director, Kerry Alholm. “Our goal is to provide children with the tools they need for a future of success by blending structured academics with creative play in a supportive environment.”

The Gardner School of Ravenswood is located at 1525 W. Wilson Ave. Operating hours are 7:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday through Friday. To schedule a tour or learn more about enrollment, visit https://www.thegardnerschool.com/schools/the-gardner-school-of-chicagoravenswood/ or call 773-948-7543.

# # #

About The Gardner School

Founded in 2004, The Gardner School is an academically focused preschool for children ages six weeks through five years old. Headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee, The Gardner School has 39 locations in Colorado, Illinois, Kentucky, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Ohio, Tennessee, Virginia, and Washington, D.C. For more information about The Gardner School, please visit thegardnerschool.com.

About NAEYC

The National Association for the Education of Young Children (NAEYC) is a professional membership organization that works to promote high-quality early learning for all young children, birth through age eight, by connecting early childhood practice, policy, and research. We advance a diverse, dynamic early childhood profession and support all who care for, educate, and work on behalf of young children. The association comprises nearly 60,000 individual members of the early childhood community and 52 Affiliates, all committed to delivering on the promise of high-quality early learning. Together, we work to achieve a collective vision: that all young children thrive and learn in a society dedicated to ensuring they reach their full potential.

Attachments

  • The Gardner School of Ravenswood achieves NAEYC accreditation for high-quality care and education.
  • Ravenswood is The Gardner School’s 11th campus in the Chicago area to earn NAEYC accreditation.

data.title: S&P 500 Falls 39.50 Points (0.64%)
Pursuit Attractions Price Target Cut to $50.00/Share From $54.00 by Oppenheimer
Nasdaq Composite Falls 125.96 Points (0.63%)
Hookipa Pharma Drops Poolbeg Takeover Bid
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Hookipa Pharma said it no longer intended to make an offer for U.K. pharmaceutical company Poolbeg.

Both companies previously agreed to extend the deadline for Hookipa to either make an offer for the company or walk away to Feb. 27, from Jan. 30.

The extension of deadline was intended to allow for talks to continue and due diligence to be completed.

On Jan. 2, Poolbeg said that it was in talks with Hookipa over an all-share takeover. Under the deal discussed at the time, accepting Poolbeg shareholders would have gotten 0.03 Hookipa shares for each Poolbeg share held.

The Nasdaq-listed biopharmaceutical company said it reserved the right to revisit its decision under specific circumstances. Among other circumstances, these include an agreement with Poolbeg's board, the announcement of a third-party offer for Poolbeg, or a reverse takeover by Poolbeg.

Poolbeg shares in London closed down 0.05 pence, or 1%, at 4.80 pence, and are down 32% in the year-to-date.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: DJIA Falls 554.70 Points (1.24%)
Bandwidth Shares Fall After 4Q Adjusted Earnings, Outlook Disappoint
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Shares of Bandwidth fell after the company posted lower-than-expected adjusted earnings for the fourth quarter and its full-year outlook fell short of analysts forecasts.

The stock was down 6.2% to $17.14 on Thursday. Shares have surged 37% over the past 12 months.

The enterprise cloud communications company logged a fourth-quarter net loss of $1.8 million, or 6 cents a share, compared with a loss of $10.9 million, or 42 cents a share, for the same period a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, the company's earnings per share came in at 37 cents. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast per-share adjusted earnings of 39 cents.

Revenue rose 27% to $210 million, ahead of the $203.7 million expected by Wall Street.

"Looking forward to 2025, we are guiding normalized revenue growth in the range of 8% to 11%, accompanied by continued growth in profitability and related free cash flow," Chief Financial Officer Daryl Raiford said.

Bandwidth expects revenue of $168 million to $170 million for the first quarter, and full-year revenue of $740 million to $760 million. Analysts forecast higher first-quarter revenue of $182.7 million, and full-year revenue of $761.5 million.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com

data.title: Medix has resubmitted tesofensine application to COFEPRIS
data.description:
Saniona (OMX: SANION), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, today announces that its partner Medix has resubmitted the tesofensine application dossier to COFEPRIS, as referenced in Saniona’s press release dated February 10.

"I congratulate the Medix team on this significant achievement. Following feedback from COFEPRIS, they have updated and resubmitted the complete tesofensine dossier - approximately 20,000 pages - addressing all regulatory questions and requests," said Saniona’s CEO, Thomas Feldthus.

data.title: LEE ENTERPRISES PARTNERS WITH GARY VAYNERCHUK’S VEEFRIENDS TO PRODUCE EXCLUSIVE WEEKLY COMICS
data.description:
DAVENPORT, Iowa, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —  Lee Enterprises Inc, a leading provider of local news, information and advertising across 72 markets in 25 states, today announced it has entered a partnership with VeeFriends, an entertainment company created by serial entrepreneur Gary Vaynerchuk, to produce original comics that will publish weekly on weekends across Lee Enterprises’ publications. 

VeeFriends, which shares daily comics on Instagram and TikTok, is dedicated to creating a better world through its universe of 251 characters. Each VeeFriends character connects differently with people, reflecting their likes, passions, memories, and aspirations. 

"Getting VeeFriends comics into Sunday news is a huge moment for VeeFriends,” said Vaynerchuk. “I’ve always believed in the power of storytelling, and this partnership with Lee Enterprises is another step in bringing these characters and their messages to life. I’m pumped for people to experience these comics every week and connect with the VeeFriends universe on a deeper level."

The weekly comics will feature VeeFriends characters, such as Brave Bison, Empathy Elephant, and Fearless Fairy, who use their superpowers to help humankind. VeeFriends comics will appear both online and in print starting February 23. 

To mark the debut of the Sunday comics and larger partnership, VeeFriends’ Director of Comics D.J. Coffman will join Adam Zyglis, Pulitzer Prize-winning cartoonist, live from the Buffalo News newsroom and Phil Hands of the Wisconsin State Journal for a series of online streaming events called “Draw Offs.” Beginning in March, these live events will give audiences a behind-the-scenes look at the creative process as Zyglis, Hands and Coffman draw comics live for viewers. In addition, readers will be invited to submit their own contributions through the Readers’ Choice feature, offering an interactive and engaging way for fans to participate.

Lee Enterprises is advancing its strategy to enhance readers’ experiences through world-class content partnerships, which includes this partnership with a compelling comics franchise.

“Working with VeeFriends allows us to bring more positivity and inspiration to our audiences,” said Nathan Bekke, Lee’s senior vice president of operations and audience strategy. “VeeFriends’ proven creativity in cartooning will delight our engaged readers. This first-of-its-kind collaboration enables us to deliver premium content that entertains subscribers with one of the most popular and compelling storytelling formats in newspaper and digital publishing: comics.”

About Lee Enterprises

Lee Enterprises Inc. is a trusted local news provider serving 72 markets in 25 states. With a dedication to quality journalism and digital innovation, Lee delivers valuable content and advertising solutions to its communities and partners. For more information, visit www.lee.net.

Contact: IR@lee.net (563) 383-2100

About VeeFriends

VeeFriends is an entertainment company dedicated to creating a better world through its universe of 251 characters. VeeFriends combines storytelling, content, collectibles, and community-driven experiences to ignite self-discovery. Each VeeFriends character connects differently with people, reflecting their likes, passions, memories, and aspirations. This unique and individual connection is the magic of VeeFriends and its characters. By sharing the values and messages of its characters, VeeFriends inspires deeper bonds, spreads joy and empowers individuals to be their best selves. For more information, visit https://veefriends.com/ and follow VeeFriends on YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and X.

Contact: Rachel Gunia (rachel@digitalandsavvy.com)

About Gary Vaynerchuk

Gary Vaynerchuk is a serial entrepreneur and serves as the Chair of VaynerX, the CEO of VaynerMedia, and the Creator and CEO of VeeFriends. Gary is considered one of the leading global minds on what’s next in culture, relevance, and the internet. Known as “GaryVee,” he is described as one of the most forward thinkers in business. He is a New York Times bestselling author, a sought-after public speaker, and a prolific investor in companies like Facebook, Twitter, Tumblr, Venmo, Snapchat, Coinbase, and Uber. Gary serves on the board of MikMak, Bojangles Restaurants, Global Citizen Forum, and Pencils of Promise. He is also a longtime Well Member of charity: water.

Contact: Maha Abouelenein (maha.abouelenein@vaynermedia.com)

###

Attachment

Lee Enterprises

563-383-2100

IR@lee.net

data.title: Palantir Technologies Software Offerings Could Benefit From Defense Budget Cuts, Wedbush Says
data.description:
Palantir Technologies stock was pressured by the Trump administration announcement to cut defense spending, but Palantir's software offerings could benefit from the move," Wedbush said Thursday.

Analysts, including Daniel Ives, said Palantir critics, who have been skeptical of the stock's rise, are now using these budget cuts as a new bearish approach, causing a sharp selloff when the news broke. However, the brokerage expects these cuts to actually benefit Palantir. The company's "unique software approach" may help it secure more IT contracts from the Pentagon, not less.

The analysts added that the increased artificial intelligence investments under the Trump Administration should benefit Palantir as more organizations adopt AI infrastructure. The company is in a positioned to take advantage of the growing federal spending on AI. It has received FedRAMP high certification for its cloud services, allowing it to sell products across the entire US government.

"We believe Palantir could be a trillion market cap over the coming years and shaping up to be the next Oracle or Salesforce as the AI Revolution plays out...we view this latest worry as noise given where we see Palantir's bright AI-driven future," according to the note.

Wedbush kept its outperform rating and $120 price target. Palantir shares fell 10% in recent trading.

data.title: Intl Flavors & Fragrances Is Maintained at Outperform by Oppenheimer
AMETEK INC/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AMETEK INC/, a leading global manufacturer of electronic instruments and electromechanical devices, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive landscape. This comprehensive summary provides insights into AMETEK's achievements and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $6,941.2 million, an increase of $344.2 million or 5.2% compared with 2023, driven by a 7% increase from acquisitions, partially offset by a 2% organic sales decline.

Operating Income: $1,779.6 million, an increase of $72.1 million or 4.2% compared with 2023, with operating margins slightly decreasing to 25.6% from 25.9% in 2023.

Net Income: $1,376.1 million, an increase of $62.9 million or 4.8% compared with 2023, reflecting higher pension income and lower acquisition-related due diligence expense.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: $5.93, an increase of $0.26 or 4.5% compared with 2023, indicating improved profitability per share.

Gross Profit: Not explicitly stated, but derived from the difference between Net Sales and Cost of Sales, which was $4,464.7 million in 2024.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: AMETEK's Electronic Instruments Group (EIG) achieved net sales of $4,659.9 million in 2024, with a 0.8% increase from 2023, driven by a 2% increase from acquisitions. The Electromechanical Group (EMG) reported net sales of $2,281.3 million, marking a 15.6% increase, primarily due to a 20% increase from acquisitions.

Geographical Performance: In 2024, 51% of EIG’s net sales were to customers outside the United States, with significant growth in Europe and Asia. EMG also saw 41% of its net sales from international markets, reflecting a strong global presence.

New Product Launches: In 2024, AMETEK introduced a range of new products across its businesses, enhancing its highly differentiated product portfolio. This includes advanced laser-based projection and inspection systems from the acquisition of Virtek Vision International.

New Production Launches: AMETEK expanded its manufacturing capabilities with facilities in China, Czechia, Malaysia, Mexico, and Serbia, supporting its Operational Excellence initiatives and increasing international sales.

Future Outlook: AMETEK aims for high single-digit annual percentage growth in sales and double-digit growth in earnings per share, focusing on strategic acquisitions, global market expansion, and new product development to drive future growth.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: AMETEK's strategic initiatives in 2024 focused on Operational Excellence, Strategic Acquisitions, Global & Market Expansion, and New Products. The company completed the acquisition of Virtek Vision International for $117.5 million, enhancing its capabilities in advanced laser-based projection and inspection systems. Additionally, AMETEK continued to invest in research, development, and engineering, spending $371.9 million, with 27% of sales coming from products introduced in the past three years.

Capital Management: In 2024, AMETEK repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of its common stock for $212.0 million and increased its quarterly cash dividend by 12% to $0.28 per share. The company reduced its total debt by $1,189.7 million, bringing the net debt-to-capital ratio down to 15.0% from 25.0% in 2023. AMETEK also paid in full a $300 million senior note at maturity. The company maintained a strong liquidity position with $2,020.3 million available under its revolving credit facility.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, AMETEK established a commercial paper program in January 2025, allowing for the issuance of up to $2.3 billion in short-term notes to provide additional financing flexibility for acquisitions and other strategic purposes. The Board of Directors approved an 11% increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $0.31 per share and authorized a new $1.25 billion share repurchase program, replacing the previous $1 billion authorization. The company plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and investment in new product development to drive growth.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Landscape: AMETEK operates in highly competitive industries, which may adversely affect its results of operations or ability to expand its business. The principal elements of competition include product technology, quality, service, distribution, and price. The company faces competition from both individual producers and vertically integrated manufacturers, some of which have greater resources.

Market Risks: AMETEK faces several risks related to its operations, including the potential for economic downturns in key markets such as aerospace and defense, oil and gas, and power markets, which could adversely affect its business. The company's growth is partly dependent on the growth of these markets, and any decline or lower-than-expected growth could diminish demand for its products and services. Additionally, AMETEK's international sales and operations are subject to risks such as trade restrictions, regulatory changes, and currency fluctuations, which could negatively impact its financial statements. The company also faces risks related to compliance with export laws and potential improper conduct by employees or partners, which could damage its reputation and lead to legal penalties.

Operational Challenges: In 2024, AMETEK achieved record sales and earnings, driven by contributions from recent acquisitions and Operational Excellence initiatives. However, the company experienced a 2% organic sales decline, highlighting challenges in maintaining organic growth. The company continues to focus on strategic acquisitions, global market expansion, and new product development to drive future growth. Management has identified customer inventory normalization in automation and engineered solutions as a factor impacting organic sales.

Market Risk Management: AMETEK's primary market risks include fluctuations in interest rates and foreign currency exchange rates. The company addresses these risks through its operating and financing activities, aiming to balance non-functional currency denominated assets and liabilities to minimize foreign exchange impacts. The company is exposed to significant exchange rate risks with currencies such as the Euro, British pound, Japanese yen, Chinese renminbi, Canadian dollar, and Mexican peso. A hypothetical ten percent adverse movement in these rates is not expected to have a material impact on future earnings, although actual effects may differ.

SEC Filing: AMETEK INC/ [ AME ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: Brent Crude Extends Uptick
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures extended their early gain to $77 per barrel on Thursday, supported by a weaker dollar and lingering risks of lower supply.

OPEC+ delegates indicated they may delay supply increases, citing concerns of a fragile market as members of the cartel previously struggled to cut output to target.

Supply was also pressured earlier in the week after a Ukrainian drone hit the Russian portion of the Caspian Pipeline Consortium, which is responsible for about 80% of Kazakh oil exports.

In the meantime, a softer dollar raised demand for oil by major importers in dollar-denominated exchanges.

On the other hand, the latest data by the EIA showed that crude oil stockpiles in the US rose by 4.6 million barrels in the second week of February, more than market expectations of a 3 million barrel increase, marking the fourth consecutive build.
